_meta.description: PME MTL — Montréal's local economic development network. Provides loans, grants, and free advisory services. Key programs: Young Business Fund ($20K), Business Startup Loan, Innovation Fund.

plain_language_summary: PME MTL is Montréal's network of local economic development centres. They offer loans, some grants, and — critically — FREE business advisory services. If you're starting a business in Montréal, PME MTL should be one of your first calls. Their advisors help you navigate permits, funding applications, and business planning at no cost.
location_required: Montréal only — must be operating or planning to operate in Montréal

programs.interest_rate: Low — typically prime + 2% or fixed low rate (verify current rate)
programs.plain_language: A $15,000 loan plus a $5,000 grant for young Montréal entrepreneurs. The $5,000 you keep — the $15,000 you repay. Strong fit for young entrepreneurs who need startup capital.

age.note: Must be 35 or under at time of application

eligibility.business_stage: Startup or early stage (within first years of operation)

immigration_status.note: PME MTL has more flexible immigration status rules than some federal programs — work permit holders may qualify. Verify with local PME MTL advisor.
immigration_status.advantage: This program is more accessible to work permit holders than Futurpreneur

eligibility.business_plan: Required — PME MTL advisors can help you prepare one for free

amount.note: Varies based on business plan and needs

programs.plain_language: A flexible startup loan for Montréal businesses at any age. Not age-restricted like the Young Entrepreneurs Fund.

immigration_status.note: More flexible — contact PME MTL directly to confirm

programs.plain_language: PME MTL's 6 local offices across Montréal provide free one-on-one advisory services in multiple languages. You can get help with: business plan preparation, funding applications, permit navigation, financial projections, and connecting with other resources. This is available to EVERYONE in Montréal regardless of immigration status or age.
what_advisors_help_with: Finding the right funding programs for your situation
Connecting you with other organizations (YES Montréal, CDEC, sector organizations)

immigration_status.eligible: All — including work permit holders, students, new arrivals

programs.languages: French, English, Spanish, Arabic, and others through interpreters
programs.how_to_access: Book an appointment online or call your nearest PME MTL office

what_you_need_to_apply: Business plan (PME MTL advisors can help you prepare one)
Proof of Montréal address or business location

timeline.advisory_services: Typically within 1-2 weeks to get first appointment

common_mistakes: Not using the free advisory services before applying for funding — advisors dramatically increase application success rates
Applying for financing before the business plan is solid — come to PME MTL first, they'll help you get ready
Not knowing your local PME MTL office — each borough has a different office
Missing the Young Entrepreneurs Fund age cutoff — apply before you turn 36
